extracted_rules.resource: Protection from legal or physical liability

extracted_rules.reasoning: Achilles explicitly exempts the heralds from blame or retaliation for Agamemnon's actions, identifying them as protected 'messengers of Zeus and men' who cannot be held responsible for the orders they carry out.
extracted_rules.specificity_reasoning: This rule applies to a specific professional class (heralds) acting in an official capacity.

extracted_rules.resource: Right to retain allocated war booty

extracted_rules.reasoning: Agamemnon asserts the right of the supreme commander to forcibly take back or redistribute 'prizes' (booty) already assigned to other leaders/soldiers if his own share is diminished, indicating that individual property rights over war spoils are subordinate to the king's status.
extracted_rules.specificity_reasoning: This affects the high-ranking warriors and leaders within the army, a substantial but not universal group.

extracted_rules.resource: Right to command attention and obedience in council
